MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON APR-29-2021 IN 206, DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. ZACHARY POWELL, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA COURTCALL. MARLENE FLORES, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT VIA COURTCALL. THE COURT DID NOT RECEIVE COURTESY COPIES OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION. AFTER ARGUMENT, THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 5/3/21 IS VACATED AND THE NEW TRIAL DATE IS 11/1/21 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206. ALL PRETRIAL DEADLINES, DISCOVERY AND MOTION PRACTICE INCLUDING 2034 CCP EXPERT DISCOVERY SHALL BE GOVERNED BY THE NEW TRIAL DATE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT AND MS. FLORES TO SERVE THE ORDER. NOTICE WAIVED BY PARTIES. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; COURT REPORTER: REBECCA REUTENAUER, CSR#14220, E-MAIL: REBECCARR320@YAHOO.COM, PHONE #(209)623-9899. (206)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 302, MAY-17-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION IS GRANTED. WITHIN 10 DAYS, COUNSEL FOR THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER IN GOOD FAITH AND SELECT A DATE NO LATER THAN JUNE 25, 2021, FOR PLAINTIFF JAMI S. HARRISON TO BE DEPOSED. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT AARON@MINNISANDSMALLETS.COM (CC'ING ALL PARTIES). JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff, And Declaration Of Marlene Flores In Support Thereof
Off Calendar May-17-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. THOMAS DREBLOW, Esq. of Knight Law Group, LLP (Tel: 310-552-2250) for Plaintiff Jami S. Harrison;
Atty. JONATHAN SHUGART, Esq. of Erskine Law Group, APC (Tel: 949-777-6032) for Defendant General Motors LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for parties authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion is granted. Within 10 days, counsel for the parties shall meet and confer in good faith and select a date no later than June 25, 2021, for Plaintiff Jami S. Harrison to be deposed.

Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com (cc'ing all parties).

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant, And Request For Sanctions First Set Of Request For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Mar-10-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Deepak Devabose for Plaintiff.
Jonathan Shugart (jshugart@erskinelaw.com) of Erskine Law for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAMI HARRISON's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Defendant, And Request For Sanctions First Set Of Request For Production Of Documents:

PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO CERTAIN REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FROM DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC {HEREAFTER "GM" OR "DEFENDANT) IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:
1. GM'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST NO. 25 ARE OVERRULED. GM IS ORDERED TO DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FRON THE DATE OF THIS ORDER ALL TECHNICAL SERVICE BULLETINS ("TSB") ISSUED FOR VEHICLES OF THE SAME YEAR, MAKE , AND MODEL AND WHICH COVER ANY OF THE CONDITIONS, DEFECTS, AND NONCONFORMATIES FOR WHICH PLAINTIFF PRESENTED THE SUBJECT VEHICLE FOR REPAIR.
2. GM'S OBJECTTIONS TO REQUEST NO. 26 ARE OVERRULED. GM IS ORDERED TO DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER DOCUMENTS SUFFICIENT TO IDENTIFY ALL RECALLS WHICH HAVE BEEN ISSUED FOR VEHICLES OF THE SAME YEAR,, MAKE , AND MODEL OF PLAINTIFF'S VEHICLE WHICH RECALLS WERE BASED ON THE SAME CONDITIONS, DEFECTS, AND NONCONFORMATIES FOR WHICH PLAINTIFF PPRESENTED THE SUBJECT VEHICLE FOR REPAIR.
3. GM'S OBJECTIONS TO REQUESTS NOS 30 & 31 ARE OVERRULED. GM IS ORDERED TO DELIVER TO PLAINTIFF WITHIN THIRTY (30) DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS TO THE EXTENT THEY EXIST.
4. GM'S OBJECTION TO REQUESTS NOS 32 & 33 ARE SUSTAINED.
5. THE COURT WILL TAKE THE SANCTIONS REQUEST UNDER SUBMISSION.

Plaintiff's counsel to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling leaving the sanctions line blank, in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to rennepar85@gmail.com

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
